rationale: Both independently rediscover the latent gap (the diffusion-transformer hunyuan_image3_transformer.py still calling the removed get_cache_scale), but X grounds it purely in verified PR-time-tree evidence while Y's parallel finding cites an unverifiable/likely-inaccurate '(see #4810)' resolution detail, denting its precision. X is more systematically actionable (per-hunk sweep, explicit blocker/major/minor tags with concrete remediation), though one of its minor findings about the AutoWeightsLoader comment self-contradicts its own earlier validation. Y better mirrors the ground-truth reviewer's substantive content (delegated-vs-direct design distinction verified against real vLLM source, correct APPROVE verdict matching the actual merged outcome) giving it a slight recall edge, but X's stronger grounding and actionability tip the overall balance narrowly in its favor.
